desc: Knysna sits on a lagoon between forest and coast, making it a flexible Garden Route stop for outdoor activities, oysters, and scenic drives.
hls: Lagoon cruises and Knysna Heads viewpoints
Forest trails in indigenous Afrotemperate woodland
Strong self-drive base between Cape Town and Tsitsikamma
Mild shoulder weather with fewer crowds than midsummer

todo.name: Knysna Heads viewpoint circuit
todo.desc: Visit both east and west head lookouts for lagoon-mouth panoramas.

todo.name: Featherbed Nature Reserve cruise + hike
todo.desc: Take the lagoon ferry and guided reserve walk.

todo.name: Forest cycle or walk in Jubilee Creek
todo.desc: Plan a half day in indigenous forest trails and picnic sites.

todo.name: Oyster tasting at waterfront
todo.desc: Try local oyster farms paired with Garden Route wines.

todo.name: Brenton-on-Sea sunset
todo.desc: Drive to Brenton beach for sunset and dune walks.

todo.name: Sedgefield market morning
todo.desc: Pair weekend market browsing with nearby estuary boardwalks.

wifi.note: Strong in town properties; patchier in remote forest lodges.

prac.fltNote: Most UK/Ireland travelers fly nonstop to Cape Town or Johannesburg, then connect by domestic flight to George or self-drive; December-January school holidays create major fare and hotel pressure.
prac.currency: South Africa local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Wildfire risk rises in hot windy spells; monitor Garden Route road advisories in summer.
Self-drive is easiest, but N2 incident delays can impact onward schedules—avoid tight airport connections.
Some lagoon and ocean activities are weather-dependent and can cancel on high-wind days.

sweet: Oct-Nov and Mar-May for mild weather and better value than peak South African summer.
